summary: Iteration 1: Tested backend (pytest) and frontend (Playwright) of Resume Match Analyzer. Backend 8/8 tests pass — POST /api/analyze with DOCX/PDF/TXT all return valid AnalysisResult, validation errors (400) for unsupported file, short JD, empty file, GET /api/analyses/{id} persists and returns 404 for missing. Frontend landing UI fully verified (hero title, STEP 01, sample JD load, DOCX upload, analyze-btn enable/disable, loading-view transition). End-to-end frontend flow into dashboard could not be visually confirmed because the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY started returning 403 'FREE_USER_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_DENIED' on the second/third browser-driven attempt (after pytest had already used up several LLM calls). Frontend error path (toast + reset to landing) does work. Dashboard component code contains all required data-testids.

minor.issue: logger is referenced inside analyze() (line 218/224/236/243) but logging.basicConfig + logger = getLogger(__name__) is defined later in the module (line 267-271). Works at runtime because module-level statements all run before requests, but order is brittle — move logger setup above route definitions.

integration_issues.issue: During Playwright e2e the LLM call returned 502 (upstream 403 FREE_USER_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_DENIED from Emergent LLM platform) after multiple prior successful calls in the same window, so dashboard view rendering could not be visually verified end-to-end. The frontend gracefully toast-errors and resets to landing — that path works.

action_items: Investigate Emergent LLM key 403 'FREE_USER_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_DENIED'. The key works for ~5-8 calls then starts returning 403 — likely a free-tier external-access rate cap. Main agent should verify quota/upgrade, OR the user must run the app from within Emergent platform.

(Minor) Move `logging.basicConfig` and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` ABOVE the route handlers in /app/backend/server.py so logger is defined at definition time, not relying on import-order coincidence.
(Optional) Main agent can self-test the dashboard view in the browser once LLM quota refreshes — full e2e from landing → loading → dashboard → reset is wired correctly in code.
critical_code_review_comments: logger ordering in server.py: logger is used inside analyze() but declared at the bottom of the file. Functional today, fragile tomorrow.
server.py `analyze` route does work and persists to MongoDB; _id is correctly stripped on GET via projection {_id:0}.

EMERGENT_LLM_KEY external rate limiting will block any prod use outside the Emergent platform — surface a friendlier error in the UI when 502 happens (currently uses generic 'AI analysis failed' detail).

success_rate.frontend: 85% (UI verified, LLM-dependent dashboard render blocked by upstream 403)

seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/resume.docx and /tmp/r2.docx via python-docx in test scripts. Inserted ~3 analysis records into MongoDB collection `analyses` during pytest runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid — reuse /app/backend/tests/test_analyzer.py. The Emergent LLM key intermittently returns 403 FREE_USER_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_DENIED when invoked from outside the Emergent platform; back-to-back POST /api/analyze calls may fail with 502 even though the code is correct. For frontend e2e, run only ONE analyze submission per session and wait ≥120s for LLM. UI shell (landing + loading + reset) works perfectly; dashboard render path is wired (data-testids exist) but couldn't be visually confirmed in this iteration due to upstream LLM throttling.
rca of the issue: Backend log shows litellm.APIError 403 with code FREE_USER_EXTERNAL_ACCESS_DENIED message='Free users can only use Universal Key from within Emergent platform'. The backend correctly maps this to HTTP 502 with detail. Frontend then surfaces a sonner toast and resets phase to landing. Repro: hit POST /api/analyze ~5-10 times in quick succession from outside the Emergent platform — eventually returns 502. Mitigation: upgrade plan or run from within Emergent platform; otherwise add retry-with-backoff in frontend and a clearer rate-limit message.
